subject: Databases
title: Second Normal Form (2NF)
description: A quick win: understand Second Normal Form (2NF).

hook.text: An order_items table has columns: order_id, product_id, product_name, quantity. Product name depends only on product_id.

buildup.text: 2NF removes partial dependencies. Every non-key column must depend on the entire primary key, not part of it.

discovery.text: Fix: move product_name to a Products table. Order_items keeps order_id, product_id, and quantity.

twist.text: 2NF only matters for composite keys. If your primary key is a single column, you're already in 2NF.

climax.text: Partial dependencies cause update anomalies: rename a product and miss some rows.

punchline.text: 2NF: every column depends on the whole key, not just part.

quiz.question: When does Second Normal Form (2NF) apply?
options: When a table has a composite primary key and non-key columns depend on only part of it
It applies to every table regardless of key structure
Only tables with no primary key need 2NF
